name: Bill Challis
abstract: William H. Challis (July 8, 1904 – October 4, 1994) was an American jazz arranger, best known for his association with the Paul Whiteman orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote him a letter and I said I’d love to study with you. And he said fine... He says, 'no, I want you to learn the Schillinger Method.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Chauncey Morehouse) does not speak about Bill Challis. The interviewer mentions Bill Challis as an arranger who joined the group, but the interviewee provides no personal interaction or relationship details regarding Challis.

about_quotes.speaker: Zilner T. Randolph
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Who was your influence? Did you ever hear any of the arrangements that Bill Challis made? [INTERVIEWEE] That who? ... Bill Challis.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: I understand that he wrote arrangements for Fletcher Henderson... what arrangements I played in Henderson's band that were written by Challis

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Cullum
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Challis went over and took them down and I think that he needed the money.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Cullum interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007
